what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies the session_supervisor route registry matches the HTTP dispatch literals in its source, that mcp_face.tools.sessions exports a closed OPS constant matching its dispatch and type signature, and that the bridge-session spawn command builders and consent gate behave as specified without spawning a real Claude process.
observations: Reads runtime/session_supervisor.py as text and uses regex to extract paths dispatched in do_GET and do_POST.
Performs a bidirectional check between SUPERVISOR_ROUTES and the extracted dispatched paths.
Imports mcp_face.tools.sessions and checks that OPS is a non-empty tuple with no duplicates.
Compares OPS against both the if op == ... branches in the tool source and the Literal[...] type signature.
Tests SessionSupervisor._build_bridge_session_cmd for a wider allowlist containing Bash, Edit, WebFetch, and mcp__company.
Asserts transport flags are preserved: -p, --input-format stream-json, --strict-mcp-config, and --mcp-config.
Asserts the default permission mode is acceptEdits and the floor spawn remains mcp__company only.
Tests capability narrowing: git yields Bash plus mcp__company without WebFetch; lsp yields Read and Edit.
Tests that computer and browser capabilities/tools are refused with TeachingRefusal, and that the refusal reason mentions macOS and -p.
Tests that unknown capabilities are refused loudly rather than silently dropped.
Tests spawn_bridge_session refuses without operator_consent and that the refusal string contains consent-gated.
Checks the source maps the consent refusal to HTTP 403 and that the spawn event records profile, operator_consent, and allowed_tools.
Uses a manual PASS/FAIL harness with a check helper instead of unittest or pytest.
standouts: Bidirectional drift detection between declared SUPERVISOR_ROUTES and actual dispatch literals.
Closed OPS verification spanning export, runtime dispatch, and Literal type signature.
Bridge-session profile acceptance testing without a live subprocess, including capability narrowing and host/rail boundary refusals.
Consent-gate test that instantiates the supervisor via __new__ with a fake store and an RLock to avoid real initialization.
conventions: Frames tests around CONTRACT-FORMAT.md section obligations in the module docstring.
Uses a lightweight custom check helper and global counters rather than a standard test framework.
Reads source files as plain text and regex-parses implementation details.
Manipulates sys.path to import runtime and mcp_face modules.
concerns: Regex source parsing is brittle to formatting changes such as whitespace, f-strings, or path comparisons not matching the expected patterns.
Hardcodes external CLI flag strings and file paths, coupling the test to a specific command shape.
Manual harness and sys.exit reduce composability with standard CI test runners and reporting.
Bypassing __init__ with __new__ and a monkey-patched lock may break if the class initialization assumptions change.
Global PASS/FAIL state makes parallel or imported execution unreliable.
notes: A contract-corpus acceptance test that uses source regex and static command-builder assertions to keep the supervisor route registry, tool OPS vocabulary, and bridge-session spawn profile in sync with their implementations.
questions: Where is the exact contract shape of SUPERVISOR_ROUTES documented outside this file?
What is the canonical set of transport-head flags and where is that contract defined?
What is the complete capability-to-tools mapping and where is it maintained?
How is TeachingRefusal defined and what other refusal reasons exist?
What is the intended CI invocation and failure-reporting format?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of verifying §9.3 route registry, §9.2 closed OPS, and L-FOUND-R1prime bridge-session profile matches the file's behavior; it additionally relies on source regex and static method tests rather than runtime integration.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create machine-inventory teeth that keep code-side registries and spawn profiles honest without requiring a live Claude session, ensuring advertised routes and operations match reality and that security boundaries refuse loudly.
novelty: It combines source-level regex drift detection for both a route registry and a tool OPS vocabulary with command-builder acceptance tests for a wider bridge-session capability profile and a consent gate.
contribution: A standalone acceptance harness that checks declared supervisor routes against dispatch literals, checks exported tool OPS against dispatch and type signatures, and exercises bridge-session command construction and refusal behavior without spawning a subprocess.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for supervisor route registry, closed tool OPS vocabulary, and bridge-session spawn profile using source regex and static command-builder assertions.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce that advertised supervisor routes and tool operations match actual dispatch and that bridge-session capability and permission boundaries refuse loudly before any subprocess work.

symbols.does: Manual assertion primitive that increments global PASS/FAIL counters and prints a pass or fail line.
symbols.description: The check function is the test harness's central assertion helper. It takes a name, a boolean condition, and an optional detail string, updates global PASS or FAIL counters, and prints a checkmark or cross line so the script can exit 0 only when all checks pass.

symbols.does: Empty placeholder class used to create a minimal supervisor instance for the consent-gate test.
symbols.description: _FakeStore is a no-op class that stands in for the real store dependency when the test constructs a SessionSupervisor instance via __new__ and manually attaches an RLock, allowing the consent-gate check to run without triggering full initialization, threads, or subprocess work.
